content: We can structure an LLM agent’s self-reflection process as a dynamic, iterative pruning and expansion of a personal semantic network built from the agent’s own past outputs and interactions, where each self-reflection step strengthens connections between conflicting concepts or underdeveloped ideas to resolve inconsistencies in the agent’s stored knowledge. This turns static semantic knowledge representation into an active, self-improving cycle that aligns the agent’s internal knowledge graph with its observed performance.
